title: Coral Reef Office Installation
content:
This stunning 300-gallon reef aquarium was designed and installed for the reception area of OceanView Financial Services. The client requested a showpiece installation that would create a memorable first impression for visitors while reflecting their company's commitment to environmental sustainability.

Project Challenges

The main challenges for this installation included:

  • Creating a self-sustaining ecosystem requiring minimal maintenance
  • Designing a system that could be monitored remotely
  • Ensuring noise levels remained appropriate for an office environment
  • Installing the system with minimal disruption to business operations
  • Creating a layout visible from multiple viewing angles

Our Solution

We designed a custom peninsula-style aquarium that serves as a room divider between the waiting area and main reception. The system features:

  • Custom-built 300-gallon rimless glass aquarium with overflow system
  • State-of-the-art filtration housed in a sound-insulated cabinet
  • Automated water testing and dosing system
  • Remote monitoring capability via smartphone app
  • Custom LED lighting programmed to simulate natural reef conditions
  • Carefully selected coral species requiring compatible care parameters
  • Automated feeding system for fish population

Results

The completed installation has become the centerpiece of the office, drawing compliments from clients and staff alike. The automated systems have performed flawlessly, requiring only bi-weekly maintenance visits from our team. The client reports increased visitor engagement, with many clients specifically commenting on the beauty of the aquarium during their visits.
excerpt: A stunning 300-gallon reef aquarium designed as a peninsula display for a corporate office reception area, featuring automated maintenance systems and remote monitoring.

title: Nature-Inspired Home Biotope
content:
This project involved creating an authentic South American biotope aquarium for a private residence. The client, an avid nature enthusiast and traveler, wanted to recreate the Amazon River basin ecosystem they had visited during their travels.

Project Vision

The client provided photographs from their Amazon expedition and requested a living recreation of the underwater environment they had glimpsed while snorkeling in tributary streams. They specifically wanted:

  • An accurate representation of Amazonian biotope
  • Only fish species native to the specific region
  • Plants indigenous to the Amazon basin
  • Natural materials including authentic driftwood and leaf litter
  • Water parameters matching the soft, tannin-rich waters of the region

Our Approach

We designed a 150-gallon custom aquarium system that meticulously recreates an Amazonian stream environment:

  • Custom-built shallow, wide aquarium to maximize surface area
  • Specialized filtration system to maintain soft, acidic water conditions
  • Carefully sourced Amazonian driftwood and botanicals
  • Substrate mix replicating riverbed materials
  • Specialized lighting to simulate dappled sunlight through forest canopy
  • Gentle current system mimicking natural water flow patterns
  • Carefully selected fish community including rare tetras, dwarf cichlids, and catfish species

Results

The completed biotope has matured into a stunning representation of an Amazonian stream. The natural processes have established a balanced ecosystem where fish display natural behaviors rarely seen in conventional aquariums. The client reports spending hours observing the tank, noting that it brings back vivid memories of their Amazon adventures.
excerpt: An authentic 150-gallon South American biotope aquarium recreating the Amazon River basin ecosystem with indigenous plants, fish species, and natural materials.

title: Restaurant Aquawall Installation
content:
The Ocean Grill seafood restaurant approached us to create a dramatic aquatic feature that would enhance their dining atmosphere while reflecting their ocean-to-table concept. After consultation, we designed a stunning 20-foot living aquawall that serves as both room divider and visual centerpiece.

Design Considerations

This commercial installation required careful planning to address several unique challenges:

  • Creating a system that could operate reliably in a busy restaurant environment
  • Designing maintenance access that wouldn't disrupt dining operations
  • Ensuring proper sound insulation for pump and filtration equipment
  • Selecting appropriate lighting that complemented the restaurant's ambiance
  • Choosing hardy fish and coral species that could thrive with consistent care
  • Incorporating the restaurant's branding elements into the design

Technical Implementation

The final installation features:

  • Custom 20' × 6' saltwater aquawall with viewing from both sides
  • Reinforced support structure integrated into the building architecture
  • Commercial-grade filtration system housed in basement equipment room
  • Redundant life support systems with emergency backup
  • Programmable LED lighting synchronized with restaurant hours
  • Remote monitoring system with alert capabilities
  • Hidden maintenance access panels for minimal disruption

Impact

Since installation, the restaurant has reported a 30% increase in reservation requests specifically for tables near the aquawall. The vibrant colors of the fish and coral create a dynamic visual experience that changes throughout the evening as lighting adjusts. The system has performed flawlessly despite the challenging environment, requiring only our standard maintenance visits.
excerpt: A dramatic 20-foot saltwater aquawall designed as a room divider for an upscale seafood restaurant, featuring viewing from both sides and remote monitoring systems.

title: Pediatric Dental Office Aquarium
content:
Bright Smiles Pediatric Dentistry commissioned us to create a child-friendly aquarium installation that would help reduce anxiety in their young patients. The goal was to create an engaging, colorful underwater world that would distract children during their wait and create positive associations with dental visits.

Project Requirements

This specialized installation needed to meet several specific criteria:

  • Child-safe design with secure access and viewing areas
  • Bright, engaging fish selection appealing to children
  • Themed elements that complemented the office's underwater decor
  • Durable construction to withstand high-traffic environment
  • Simple maintenance routine manageable by office staff
  • Educational elements to spark curiosity and learning

Our Solution

We designed a 180-gallon custom aquarium with these special features:

  • Built-in viewing bubbles at child height for immersive viewing
  • Custom background with hidden 'treasure hunt' elements
  • Selection of colorful, active fish species safe for viewing by children
  • Automated feeding system with scheduled demonstrations during peak hours
  • Integrated information panels with fun fish facts
  • Special lighting effects including 'treasure chest' that periodically opens with bubbles
  • Simplified filtration system with clear maintenance protocols

Outcome

The aquarium has been tremendously successful in transforming the waiting experience. Staff report that children now look forward to visits specifically to see the fish, and parents appreciate the positive distraction. The office has even incorporated the aquarium into their care approach, using fish-related stories and activities to help explain dental procedures to young patients.
excerpt: A 180-gallon child-friendly aquarium designed for a pediatric dental office, featuring interactive elements, colorful fish, and special viewing bubbles at child height.

title: Japanese Garden Pond Renovation
content:
This project involved the complete renovation of an existing 2,500-gallon outdoor koi pond that had fallen into disrepair. The homeowners wanted to restore and enhance their Japanese-inspired garden centerpiece while addressing persistent water quality and filtration issues.

Initial Assessment

Our evaluation of the existing pond revealed several critical issues:

  • Inadequate filtration system for the pond volume and fish load
  • Leaking liner causing water loss and ground saturation
  • Poor circulation creating dead zones and algae problems
  • Outdated pumping system consuming excessive electricity
  • Overgrown aquatic plants disrupting water balance
  • Insufficient protection from predators

Renovation Process

The comprehensive renovation included:

  • Complete draining and temporary relocation of koi collection
  • Excavation and reshaping of pond contours for improved flow
  • Installation of new EPDM rubber liner with protective underlayment
  • Construction of natural stone edges and waterfall feature
  • Installation of energy-efficient pump and UV sterilization system
  • Creation of dedicated bog filter with specialized aquatic plants
  • Addition of bottom drains and skimmers for improved maintenance
  • Installation of aeration system for improved oxygen levels
  • Strategic underwater lighting for nighttime viewing
  • Construction of partial cover structure for shade and predator protection

Results

The renovated pond has transformed into the serene centerpiece the clients envisioned. Water clarity has dramatically improved, and the koi collection is thriving in their enhanced environment. The new filtration system has reduced maintenance requirements by 70%, while the energy-efficient pump has lowered operating costs significantly. The clients report spending much more time enjoying their outdoor space, particularly in the evening when the new lighting creates a magical atmosphere.
excerpt: Complete renovation of a 2,500-gallon Japanese koi pond featuring natural filtration, energy-efficient systems, and enhanced aesthetics with traditional elements.
